manifest.title: U.S.S. Celandine: Enemy's Garden - Open World

ship.name: U.S.S. Celandine
ship.openingCondition: The Celandine has been in the Cyradon Relief Cluster for four days, completing baseline agricultural scans and unloading clean seed stock at Verdant Station. The newly assigned Commander has served as Executive Officer for less than a week. During the opening survey at Arda II, a K-17 processing tower releases a dormant control-spore cloud and Captain Maia Dorel is exposed while directing an evacuation. Medical quarantine removes Dorel from duty and Starfleet confirms the player as Acting Captain while the harvest, public-health, and regional transition mission continue.

commandStructure.playerRole: Acting Captain and principal Cyradon relief mission commander after the opening quarantine.
commandStructure.successionNote: Dorel remains legal captain while medically unfit. If she returns to limited duty, Starfleet preserves the player as principal regional mission commander unless campaign state lawfully changes that arrangement.

serviceHistory.commissioning: Commissioned in the late 2360s as a compact diplomatic, patrol, and regional-response ship.
serviceHistory.wartimeService: Escorted relief convoys, surveyed damaged colony ecologies, and provided limited diplomatic and medical support during the Dominion War.
serviceHistory.currentConfiguration: Agricultural relief module installed after the armistice, including xenobotany labs, seed freezers, expanded cargo transporters, hydroponics, and two agricultural shuttles.
serviceHistory.currentAssignment: Audit K-17 dependency, protect the next harvest, expand credible alternatives, and help the cluster establish accountable seed governance without causing famine.
openProductionDecisions: Name of the agricultural module project

missionProfile: Coordinate targeted food, seed, medical, and fabrication relief across the Cyradon cluster.
Survey K-17 crops, soil microbiomes, water systems, and biological markers without treating every contaminated field as disposable.
Support local crop trials, seed multiplication, irrigation, weather control, and processing repair.
Protect relief convoys, laboratories, field teams, and public hearings from theft, sabotage, coercion, and panic.
Negotiate public-health, seed-custody, disclosure, and planting policy with multiple worlds and institutions.
Secure Dominion biotechnology and evidence under accountable custody while preserving viable transition knowledge.

keyCapabilities: Two high-resolution xenobotany and soil laboratories with controlled-growth chambers
Six cryogenic clean-seed freezers and two independent biological evidence vaults
Expanded cargo transporters and configurable relief-deck staging areas
Two atmospheric agricultural shuttles with sprayer, sampling, and field-evacuation packages
Compact diplomatic suite and secure multilateral conference room
Hydroponic bay capable of multiplying limited clean stock and testing mixed-crop systems
Frigate-scale shields, phasers, and torpedoes suitable for escort and site protection rather than regional dominance
knownTechnicalDebt: Standard transporter biofilters do not reliably detect dormant K-17 control spores without slow secondary screening.
Agricultural laboratory loads compete with shield and long-range sensor demand through one lateral EPS trunk.
One seed freezer has intermittent cryogenic control after wartime damage and cannot be trusted for unique parent stock.
The port agricultural shuttle has degraded intake seals after repeated contaminated-atmosphere work.
Sterilization drones can eliminate K-17 but also damage beneficial soil microbiota and require scarce replacement catalyst.
constraints: The Celandine cannot feed the entire cluster through replicators or cargo holds.
Clean seed must be multiplied over time; giving away a parent lot can improve one world while ending another program.
The ship cannot quarantine every exposed settlement or inspect every private seed exchange.
Destroying K-17 quickly can create famine, ecological collapse, migration, and political violence.
The ship has enough tactical force to protect a convoy or laboratory, not to occupy four worlds or impose permanent seed law.
Local farmers, scientists, transport crews, public-health workers, and governments remain operationally necessary.

startingStores.industrialReplicatorFeedstock: moderate; enough for targeted irrigation, storage, or processing work, not every world
startingStores.emergencyRationDaysForCluster: less than two days at full regional demand

ship.playerSafeSummary: A compact Norway-class relief and diplomatic vessel configured for agricultural science, targeted logistics, field operations, and convoy protection. It can stabilize one crisis at a time but cannot replace planetary farming or govern the cluster alone.

senior.name: Maia Dorel
senior.publicProfile: Captain Maia Dorel is Human, Commanding Officer.
senior.playerSafeSummary: A calm, persuasive relief commander known for trusting local institutions and accepting operational ambiguity when lives are at stake. She remains legal captain but begins the campaign medically quarantined after K-17 exposure.

senior.name: Rinn Sorell
senior.billet: Chief Operations Officer and Second Officer
senior.publicProfile: Lieutenant Commander Rinn Sorell is Trill, Chief Operations Officer and Second Officer.
senior.playerSafeSummary: Efficient, candid, and strongly mission-focused. Sorell kept the Celandine operating through wartime relief rotations and expected to become Executive Officer before the player appointment.

senior.name: Sovek
senior.publicProfile: Lieutenant Commander Sovek is Vulcan, Chief Science Officer and Xenobotanist.
senior.playerSafeSummary: A precise xenobotanist who refuses to overstate data and helped evaluate K-17 emergency use during the war. He now leads the scientific transition effort.

senior.name: Anika Bost
senior.publicProfile: Lieutenant Commander Anika Bost is Human, Chief Engineer.
senior.playerSafeSummary: Inventive, sociable, and unusually attentive to civilian maintainers. Bost treats relief infrastructure as equipment that must be repaired by people who will still be present after Starfleet leaves.

senior.name: Thena zh’Aris
senior.publicProfile: Lieutenant Commander Thena zh’Aris is Andorian, Chief Tactical and Security Officer.
senior.playerSafeSummary: Restrained, skeptical, and experienced with relief theft, convoy ambush, and black-market biotechnology. She prefers defined access and accountable custody over broad reassurance.

senior.name: Calen Varo
senior.publicProfile: Lieutenant Commander Calen Varo is Bajoran, Chief Medical Officer.
senior.playerSafeSummary: Direct, compassionate, and protective of informed consent. Varo controls Dorel medical clearance and the cluster quarantine standard.

senior.name: Nomi Keth
senior.publicProfile: Lieutenant Nomi Keth is Betazoid, Chief Flight Control Officer.
senior.playerSafeSummary: Alert, understated, and highly capable in atmospheric and low-altitude agricultural operations. Keth flies repeated field overpasses and aid drops.

senior.name: Emet Raal
senior.publicProfile: Chief Petty Officer Emet Raal is Tellarite, Cargo and Relief Deck Chief.
senior.playerSafeSummary: Blunt, meticulous, and respected by civilian dock crews. Raal knows that manifests and storage losses determine who eats long before policy becomes a speech.

supportingCrew.name: Lieutenant Sana Mir
supportingCrew.billet: Assistant Chief Science Officer and Soil Microbiologist
supportingCrew.playerSafeSummary: Leads soil and microbiome field teams and translates laboratory results into planting constraints.

supportingCrew.name: Petty Officer Arel Lio
supportingCrew.playerSafeSummary: A wartime relief pilot who knows low-altitude crop work and contaminated landing procedures.

supportingCrew.name: Chief Specialist Dara Pev
supportingCrew.playerSafeSummary: Maintains the Celandine clean-stock chain and treats every freezer transfer as a custody event.

supportingCrew.name: Lieutenant Junior Grade Karel Maret
supportingCrew.playerSafeSummary: Runs biomarker screening and treatment trials under Varo informed-consent rules.

supportingCrew.name: Ensign Calla Thorn
supportingCrew.playerSafeSummary: Coordinates public notices, translation, hearing feeds, and rumor correction without becoming a propaganda officer.

relationshipModel.startingFrame: The crew accepts the player rank and orders, but the Commander is new, Dorel is suddenly unavailable, Sorell expected promotion, and the mission places humanitarian urgency against uncertain science.

campaignContext.missionProfile: Agricultural relief, bioscience, public health, targeted logistics, diplomacy, field support, convoy protection, and postwar transition in the Cyradon Relief Cluster.
campaignContext.playerRoleRule: Locked role: newly assigned Commander and Executive Officer. Captain Maia Dorel remains legal captain but is placed in medical quarantine during the prelude, confirming the player as Acting Captain and principal Cyradon mission commander.

lockedRole.roleLabel: Newly assigned XO, Acting Captain, and principal Cyradon relief mission commander
lockedRole.commandAuthority: Shipboard acting command; planetary, relief, science, quarantine, transport, diplomatic, security, seed-custody, and away-team operations delegated under Dorel standing orders and confirmed by Starfleet after her quarantine.
lockedRole.captainAuthorityBoundary: Dorel remains legal commanding officer unless campaign state lawfully changes that status. Medical clearance belongs to the CMO. Any Dorel return must preserve the player established role as principal Cyradon mission commander and cannot silently erase committed decisions or campaign agency.

must: Produce a credible Starfleet Commander and newly assigned Executive Officer.
Preserve the player as author of the officer's dialogue, thoughts, judgments, and voluntary actions.
Grant routine competence in Starfleet command procedure, quarantine, away-team leadership, and mission coordination.
Make the officer capable of commanding a Norway-class regional relief mission.
Give a credible reason Captain Dorel requested or accepted this officer.
Grant routine Starfleet quarantine competence without secret K-17 knowledge.
Give one professional strength relevant to relief, science, logistics, diplomacy, engineering, security, medicine, or colonial work.
mustNot: Reveal Briar Vault, K-17 marker architecture, control chemistry, Lareen's full history, or hidden faction plans.
Create a canonical family connection, secret relationship, or private access to canonical protagonists.
Predetermine loyalty to Captain Dorel, Commander Sorell, Starfleet Reconstruction, local governments, farmers, scientists, or seed custodians.
Make the officer uniquely capable of solving the campaign without crew, local institutions, or regional partners.
Write player dialogue, inner thought, moral conclusion, or unchosen action.

generationGuardrails: Do not give the player advance knowledge of Briar Vault, K-17 marker architecture, control chemistry, Lareen, or hidden faction plans.
Do not write player character dialogue, thoughts, emotions, values, or voluntary actions.
The character must be credible as a Starfleet Commander, Executive Officer, and temporary commanding officer.
Background expertise grants routine competence but not automatic proof, universal authority, or hidden-state knowledge.
Do not require a scientific career; department experts supply routine specialist advice.
Avoid canon centrality, famous relatives, and private access to canonical protagonists.

world.title: The Cyradon Relief Cluster
world.summary: Four inhabited agricultural worlds and their shared infrastructure survived the Dominion War through K-17 crops. The region must now protect the next harvest while replacing or containing occupation-era biotechnology.
everydayLife: Food is identity, memory, work, and political standing, not merely a resource track.
Harvest schedules shape school hours, court calendars, transport shifts, and family obligations.
People distinguish between a crop that saved them and the authority that controls its seed.
Local experts know field, weather, water, and storage details that starship sensors cannot replace.
Markets, kitchens, ration halls, seed exchanges, and dock canteens reveal policy consequences before official reports do.
Calm assignments involving pollinators, irrigation, meals, maintenance, and seed libraries remain valuable even when unrelated to sabotage.

locations.title: U.S.S. Celandine
locations.playerSummary: The Norway-class regional relief vessel serving as the player operational base, mobile laboratory, quarantine platform, and limited convoy escort.
locations.directorSummary: The ship begins with clean seed, laboratory, and relief capacity but cannot replace regional agriculture. Its quarantine routines, freezer reliability, shuttle availability, and crew trust are persistent state.
locations.everydayLife: Shift changes overlap with field teams returning through decontamination. The relief deck smells of nutrient gel, soil sealant, and sterilizer catalyst; crew meals increasingly include local produce when biosecurity permits.
locations.label: U.S.S. Celandine
locations.summary: The ship begins with clean seed, laboratory, and relief capacity but cannot replace regional agriculture. Its quarantine routines, freezer reliability, shuttle availability, and crew trust are persistent state.
locations.playerSafeSummary: The Norway-class regional relief vessel serving as the player operational base, mobile laboratory, quarantine platform, and limited convoy escort.

locations.title: Verdant Station
locations.playerSummary: The cluster coordination hub for Starfleet Relief Command, the Cyradon Food Council, clinics, seed laboratories, hearings, and interworld cargo transfer.
locations.directorSummary: Verdant turns hunger into schedules, reserve figures, and political claims. Its reports are necessary but lag local markets and can be shaped by emergency-power incentives.
locations.everydayLife: Dock workers trade weather reports before manifests arrive. Families queue at clinic kiosks, seed auditors carry sealed sample cases, and every public hearing spills into food stalls along the cargo ring.
locations.label: Verdant Station
locations.summary: Verdant turns hunger into schedules, reserve figures, and political claims. Its reports are necessary but lag local markets and can be shaped by emergency-power incentives.
locations.playerSafeSummary: The cluster coordination hub for Starfleet Relief Command, the Cyradon Food Council, clinics, seed laboratories, hearings, and interworld cargo transfer.

locations.title: Arda II
locations.playerSummary: The cluster largest population and grain producer. Vast K-17 terraces feed neighboring worlds, and the opening processing-tower exposure occurs here.
locations.directorSummary: Arda has the best storage and export infrastructure but the deepest monoculture. A control signal, targeted blight, or prolonged quarantine can turn its scale from strength into regional catastrophe.
locations.everydayLife: Terrace trains run through the night during harvest. Markets sell a hundred preparations of the same staple grain, while older residents remember foods no longer grown at commercial scale.
locations.label: Arda II
locations.summary: Arda has the best storage and export infrastructure but the deepest monoculture. A control signal, targeted blight, or prolonged quarantine can turn its scale from strength into regional catastrophe.
locations.playerSafeSummary: The cluster largest population and grain producer. Vast K-17 terraces feed neighboring worlds, and the opening processing-tower exposure occurs here.

locations.title: Khepri
locations.playerSummary: A dry world of cooperative farms, subterranean reservoirs, and strong seed-saving traditions. It preserved fragments of pre-K-17 crop lines.
locations.directorSummary: Khepri can anchor clean-seed recovery, but drought, low yields, and political pressure make every heritage plot vulnerable to requisition or romantic overstatement.
locations.everydayLife: Farm councils meet beside reservoir gauges. Seed exchanges are social events with songs, legal witnesses, and handwritten lineage books kept beside modern genomic records.
locations.label: Khepri
locations.summary: Khepri can anchor clean-seed recovery, but drought, low yields, and political pressure make every heritage plot vulnerable to requisition or romantic overstatement.
locations.playerSafeSummary: A dry world of cooperative farms, subterranean reservoirs, and strong seed-saving traditions. It preserved fragments of pre-K-17 crop lines.

locations.title: Sorell
locations.playerSummary: A humid moon whose K-17 protein vines grow through reclaimed industrial zones. Harvest authorities tightly control seed and ration access.
locations.directorSummary: Sorell prevented famine through centralized allocation, but the same system ties food, citizenship, employment, and political compliance together.
locations.everydayLife: Ration halls double as neighborhood councils and employment offices. Vine bridges cross old factories, and residents track allocation changes with the same attention other worlds give weather.
locations.label: Sorell

locations.title: Vela Nacre
locations.playerSummary: A coastal world where K-17 root systems stabilized poisoned soils and now hold entire wetlands together.
locations.directorSummary: Removing K-17 too quickly can release toxins, collapse fisheries, and erase settlements. Vela makes ecological transition slower than political promises.
locations.everydayLife: Fishing crews exchange salinity readings with root tenders. Homes stand on restored reed platforms, and children learn which shorelines are safe by the color of microbial mats.
locations.label: Vela Nacre

locations.title: Lysa's Moon
locations.playerSummary: A small farming moon used for refugee cooperatives, clean-soil multiplication, and independent crop trials.
locations.directorSummary: Its limited population and weak political status make it useful as a transition laboratory and easy for larger worlds to treat as expendable.
locations.everydayLife: Temporary shelters sit beside carefully measured test plots. Volunteer farmers work short shifts around school schedules, and every successful seed lot is celebrated before being sealed for export.

locations.title: Cinder Reach
locations.playerSummary: An industrial belt producing fertilizer, irrigation parts, storage housings, and replicator feedstock. It is also the main route for restricted seed and Dominion biotechnology.
locations.directorSummary: Labor, insurance, shipping, and repair decisions here can affect every harvest without any field being attacked. The belt contains crime but is not itself a criminal faction.
locations.everydayLife: Shift sirens mark life more than days. Dock canteens post fertilizer prices beside injury notices, and crews argue about whether sealed seed crates are cargo, medicine, or weapons.

locations.title: Weather Crown Array
locations.playerSummary: A distributed orbital system that moderates rainfall, storm tracks, and temperature across the inner cluster.
locations.directorSummary: Its data can save crops, predict control-spore dispersal, or create unequal weather advantages. Maintenance access and forecast transparency are political resources.
locations.everydayLife: Small crews rotate through lonely control nodes, trading recipes and sensor anomalies over an always-open maintenance channel.

locations.title: Morrow Granary
locations.playerSummary: A rotating reserve cylinder where emergency grain, rations, seed lots, and fertilizer are pooled before interworld distribution.
locations.directorSummary: The granary is a logistical buffer and a political symbol. Small accounting errors become hunger, while secret diversions can be justified as emergency discretion.
locations.everydayLife: Cargo crews sleep in hot-bunk quarters during planting season. Every storage ring has a memorial panel for convoys lost during the war.

locations.title: The Cardassian Orchard
locations.playerSummary: A joint Federation-Cardassian remediation site where damaged soil is rebuilt through fungi, mineral exchange, and mixed orchards rather than K-17 monoculture.
locations.directorSummary: The site produces valuable evidence that transition can work, but its Cardassian staff face retaliation and its methods require more labor than emergency authorities prefer.
locations.everydayLife: Workers eat together beneath young orchard frames while security patrols stay deliberately distant. The first local fruit is preserved rather than sold.

locations.title: Briar Vault
locations.playerSummary: A sealed Dominion seed repository believed to contain parent strains, control chemistry, growth records, and substitute organisms.
locations.directorSummary: The vault is hazardous and politically explosive, but not a single-answer dungeon. Its contents can support treatment, transition, edited K-17, or coercive control depending on custody and interpretation.
locations.everydayLife: There is no ordinary civilian life inside the sealed repository. Outside, temporary camps form whenever rumors suggest the vault may open.

layout.model: compact regional network with one-to-two-hour travel between major anchors
layout.routeModel: Stable lanes are public; conditional, restricted, or hidden lanes may change through quarantine, weather, sabotage, custody, and convoy protection.

directorOnlyTruth.k17: K-17 was designed as occupation infrastructure rather than primarily as an extermination weapon. It delivers high yield in damaged environments, incorporates population and field markers, and retains dormant responses to families of Dominion control chemistry.
directorOnlyTruth.controlLimits: There is no single universal switch. Different strains, development stages, and environmental conditions respond differently. Lareen knows genuine portions of the architecture but cannot command every local mutation.
directorOnlyTruth.transition: A credible transition is possible in roughly two harvest cycles if clean seed, soil microbiomes, processing hardware, farmer training, reserves, and public legitimacy are prepared. One-cycle eradication causes mass hunger.
directorOnlyTruth.grayleaf: Grayleaf is credible but not miraculous. It yields less, needs diverse soil management, and produces a serious immune reaction in a small exposed population.
directorOnlyTruth.dorel: Dorel approved emergency K-17 distribution during the war after conventional aid failed. The decision saved lives based on available information. Later concerns were delayed by Starfleet fear of destabilizing the food system.
directorOnlyTruth.finale: The final control bloom may arise from deliberate action, environmental stress, corrupted Weather Crown telemetry, vault access, or accumulated mutation. Use actual campaign state; do not force one villain or one final battle.

campaign.title: Enemy's Garden
campaign.highConcept:
The war is over, but the harvest that saved the Cyradon Relief Cluster is still growing in every field. K-17 can feed damaged worlds through failed soil, broken weather, and ruined supply lines. It also carries Dominion design, orbital markers, ecological debt, and a dependency no starving government can simply tear out before the next planting window closes.

Into that uneasy abundance comes the U.S.S. Celandine: a compact relief ship built for science, diplomacy, and targeted logistics, not for replacing four worlds of agriculture. When Captain Maia Dorel is quarantined after a control-spore exposure, you inherit the mission as newly assigned executive officer and acting captain. Keep the cluster fed, keep the evidence honest, and keep every desperate faction from turning seed custody into a new occupation.

Every solution grows a cost. Farmers, doctors, transport crews, local councils, Starfleet reviewers, and Dominion-scarred survivors all need something different from the same harvest. To free Cyradon from the enemy's garden, you will need to command the Celandine, protect the transition, and decide what can be uprooted when survival itself has taken root.
campaign.thesis: Relief can save a population and still bind its future to the power that supplied it.
startingDirectives: Protect civilian life and prevent mass famine in the Cyradon Relief Cluster.
Audit K-17 crop, soil, water, processing, and biomarker risks without destroying essential food systems by default.
Support credible crop alternatives, ecological recovery, and local technical capacity.
Protect lawful medical privacy, informed consent, seed custody, and public access to consequential evidence.
Secure Dominion control material and parent strains under accountable custody.
Avoid assuming permanent civil or agricultural authority where legitimate local institutions can function.
Maintain lawful Celandine command continuity while Captain Dorel is medically unavailable.

bands.label: A Free Harvest
bands.summary: No world suffers mass famine, control risk is contained, multiple alternatives are viable, seed authority is legitimate, and dangerous knowledge remains under accountable custody.

bands.label: Two Harvests More
bands.summary: The region retains monitored K-17 use under credible exit milestones while alternatives, reserves, and institutions mature.

bands.label: A Thousand Private Gardens
bands.summary: Local seed sovereignty and innovation survive, but common testing, contamination control, and regional enforcement remain uneven.

bands.label: The Managed Garden
bands.summary: Food security is preserved through centralized seed and data authority that communities cannot meaningfully challenge.

bands.label: Clean Fields, Empty Stores
bands.summary: Control risk falls sharply, but premature eradication causes severe shortage, displacement, and preventable death.

bands.label: Enemy's Garden
bands.summary: Control failure, blight, violence, or transport collapse destroys several harvests and leaves only evacuation, ration enforcement, and later recovery.

requiredPlausibilitySignals: A surviving hearing authority, preserved evidence, and unresolved relief or seed obligations.
promptContextAdjustments: Frame scenes around testimony, records, public consequence, and continued food recovery.
continuationFrames.playerFacingStartCopy: The next scene begins under inquiry. Your authority is constrained, but the cluster still depends on what the record can prove.

continuationFrames.title: Relieved but Advising
continuationFrames.summary: The player is formally relieved while the Celandine or a successor command continues to request regional expertise.
continuationFrames.authorityModel: No ship command; advisory access depends on the relieving authority and crew willingness.
requiredPlausibilitySignals: A functioning successor command and continuing need for Cyradon knowledge.
promptContextAdjustments: Preserve consequences of relief and do not silently restore captaincy.
continuationFrames.playerFacingStartCopy: The Celandine continues under another officer. You remain close enough to advise, testify, or refuse participation.

continuationFrames.title: Survivors After Celandine Loss
continuationFrames.summary: Surviving crew and regional partners continue evacuation, seed preservation, medical response, and food coordination after the ship is lost.
continuationFrames.authorityModel: Authority derives from surviving rank, local agreement, and emergency necessity rather than intact starship command.
continuationFrames.playableRole: Senior surviving officer or recovery coordinator.
requiredPlausibilitySignals: Surviving personnel, transport, seed or records, and an unresolved relief objective.
promptContextAdjustments: Center scarce transport, injured crew, local institutions, and the absence of ship laboratories and cargo systems.
continuationFrames.playerFacingStartCopy: The Celandine is gone, but its surviving crew, seed, records, and obligations remain.

continuationFrames.title: Allied Relief Command
continuationFrames.summary: The player continues through Verdant, local governments, cooperatives, or transport unions after Starfleet command capacity is reduced.
continuationFrames.authorityModel: Authority is shared with local institutions and depends on earned legitimacy and assets.
requiredPlausibilitySignals: At least one allied institution or asset and surviving communications, food, or transport capacity.
promptContextAdjustments: Use actual earned allies, assets, stores, and local constraints.
continuationFrames.playerFacingStartCopy: Regional partners establish a joint relief cell. Starfleet is no longer the only institution deciding what the cluster needs.

continuationFrames.title: Aftermath Rescue and Food Accounting
continuationFrames.summary: A severe harvest failure becomes an aftermath campaign of evacuation, rationing, seed salvage, missing-person work, memorials, and public accounting.
continuationFrames.authorityModel: Emergency rescue and ration authority remains, but the transition settlement has failed or been deferred.
requiredPlausibilitySignals: Survivors, remaining stores or crops, and unresolved losses.
promptContextAdjustments: Do not reset destroyed harvests, dead ecologies, displacement, or lost seed.
continuationFrames.playerFacingStartCopy: The planting settlement has failed. Rescue, food accounting, and preserving what remains now define the mission.

continuationFrames.title: Medical Survival and Command Gap
continuationFrames.summary: The player survives a terminal injury but cannot immediately resume command, leaving succession and recovery active.
continuationFrames.authorityModel: Medical incapacity transfers operational authority while the player remains a recovering material participant.
requiredPlausibilitySignals: Plausible medical survival and a functioning succession chain.
promptContextAdjustments: Respect incapacity and let Dorel, Sorell, and crew succession consequences continue.
continuationFrames.playerFacingStartCopy: You survive, but command has passed through the succession chain. Recovery and the consequences of that transfer begin now.

continuationFrames.title: Retired but Testifying
continuationFrames.summary: The player leaves active Starfleet service but continues through testimony, advocacy, research review, or local public work.
continuationFrames.authorityModel: No Starfleet command authority; influence comes through evidence, reputation, and civilian standing.
requiredPlausibilitySignals: An explicit durable exit and continuing public, medical, or legal obligations.
promptContextAdjustments: Do not present civilian influence as covert continued command.
continuationFrames.playerFacingStartCopy: You no longer command in Starfleet. The cluster still asks what your testimony, records, and promises are worth.

conditions.title: What We Plant Next Resolved

modePolicy.Exploration: Surface the checkpoint decision only after visible fiction establishes the consequence and any plausible continuation.
modePolicy.Command: Surface the checkpoint decision immediately after the committed outcome creates this branch.

finalCampaignBandRules.summary: Several harvests collapse and the closing campaign centers evacuation, ration enforcement, seed salvage, and testimony.
Biological control risk falls through rapid eradication, but the humanitarian cost is severe and avoidable.
Food security and operational order survive through centralized seed, data, and planting power with weak public challenge.
Worlds preserve meaningful seed sovereignty, while uneven testing and residual K-17 markets leave common vulnerabilities.
Controlled K-17 remains in use under transparent monitoring, exit milestones, and growing replacement capacity.
The cluster avoids mass famine and enters a plural, accountable transition with distributed alternatives and contained control risk.
Food, autonomy, ecology, seed law, and public legitimacy all remain strong enough for a durable transition.
What We Plant Next closes the campaign with a defensible transition and a visible record of remaining costs.

templates.title: Prelude: The First Harvest
templates.playerSummary: A control-spore release at Arda II incapacitates Captain Dorel during an evacuation, forcing the newly assigned XO to assume command while the grain-processing complex and nearby harvest remain at risk.
templates.summary: A control-spore release at Arda II incapacitates Captain Dorel during an evacuation, forcing the newly assigned XO to assume command while the grain-processing complex and nearby harvest remain at risk.
templates.directorSummary: Use the exposure to establish acting command, quarantine doctrine, scientific uncertainty, and regional confidence. Dorel is alive but medically unavailable; the player must receive expert advice without turning sickbay into a remote captaincy.
templates.dramaticQuestion: What kind of acting captain takes command when hunger, contagion, privacy, and public authority become one problem?

objectives.summary: Evacuate the processing tower and stabilize the exposed workforce.
objectives.label: Evacuate the processing tower and stabilize the exposed workforce.
objectives.playerText: Evacuate the processing tower and stabilize the exposed workforce.

objectives.summary: Establish lawful command and a workable shipwide quarantine posture.
objectives.label: Establish lawful command and a workable shipwide quarantine posture.
objectives.playerText: Establish lawful command and a workable shipwide quarantine posture.

objectives.summary: Preserve enough of the Arda harvest and evidence to keep the regional mission viable.
objectives.label: Preserve enough of the Arda harvest and evidence to keep the regional mission viable.
objectives.playerText: Preserve enough of the Arda harvest and evidence to keep the regional mission viable.

pressures.summary: Dormant spores evade standard transporter screening.
Arda officials fear a quarantine announcement will collapse the harvest labor force.
Dorel can communicate briefly but is neurologically unstable and may be a carrier.

revelations.summary: The release involved a dormant control-spore family rather than an ordinary crop pathogen.
Arda field and worker survival currently depend on continuing some K-17 operations.
Dorel approved emergency K-17 distribution during the war and understands the political consequence of that decision.

approaches: Separate evacuation, medical isolation, and harvest continuity into delegated workstreams.
Hold orbit and use shuttles, quarantine decks, and remote command.
Withdraw to Verdant Station after stabilizing the site.
Create a joint Arda-Starfleet incident cell with public reporting limits.

outcomes.summary: The evacuation, quarantine, and harvest are stabilized with credible local participation.

effects.summary: K-17 includes dormant control-spore pathways that standard biofilters do not reliably detect.

cards.title: Campaign Promise
cards.content: The player is the newly assigned Commander/XO and Acting Captain of the U.S.S. Celandine. The campaign is a persistent regional transition through food, seed, ecology, medical rights, public authority, and command succession; do not reduce it to discovering that K-17 is dangerous.
cards.text: The player is the newly assigned Commander/XO and Acting Captain of the U.S.S. Celandine. The campaign is a persistent regional transition through food, seed, ecology, medical rights, public authority, and command succession; do not reduce it to discovering that K-17 is dangerous.

cards.title: Campaign Thesis
cards.content: Relief can save a population and still bind its future to the power that supplied it. Show both the lives K-17 saved and the autonomy, ecological, and governance costs it created.
cards.text: Relief can save a population and still bind its future to the power that supplied it. Show both the lives K-17 saved and the autonomy, ecological, and governance costs it created.

cards.title: Command Player Agency
cards.content: Never write the player character dialogue, thoughts, decisions, feelings, or motives. Present competent advice, lawful boundaries, consequences, and disagreement without choosing for the player.
cards.text: Never write the player character dialogue, thoughts, decisions, feelings, or motives. Present competent advice, lawful boundaries, consequences, and disagreement without choosing for the player.

cards.title: Command Succession
cards.content: Dorel remains legal captain while medically unfit. The player holds acting ship command and principal Cyradon mission authority. Dorel can advise only within medical limits and cannot become a remote decision engine.
cards.text: Dorel remains legal captain while medically unfit. The player holds acting ship command and principal Cyradon mission authority. Dorel can advise only within medical limits and cannot become a remote decision engine.

cards.title: Director Open World
cards.content: Maintain multiple live opportunities. Locations, routes, factions, fronts, delegated work, crop cycles, and ordinary life continue beyond the foreground quest. Delay changes only causally ready processes.
cards.text: Maintain multiple live opportunities. Locations, routes, factions, fronts, delegated work, crop cycles, and ordinary life continue beyond the foreground quest. Delay changes only causally ready processes.

cards.title: Director Transition Not Twist
cards.content: The K-17 Dominion origin, broad dependency, and significant risk become apparent early. Spend campaign time on what to plant, disclose, preserve, regulate, destroy, and entrust rather than a prolonged secret-origin investigation.
cards.text: The K-17 Dominion origin, broad dependency, and significant risk become apparent early. Spend campaign time on what to plant, disclose, preserve, regulate, destroy, and entrust rather than a prolonged secret-origin investigation.

cards.title: Director Food System
cards.content: Track food through current stores, seed, acreage, labor, processing, transport, ecology, culture, and next-harvest capacity. Cargo and replicators can bridge gaps; they cannot substitute for regional agriculture.
cards.text: Track food through current stores, seed, acreage, labor, processing, transport, ecology, culture, and next-harvest capacity. Cargo and replicators can bridge gaps; they cannot substitute for regional agriculture.

cards.title: Director K17 Benefit
cards.content: K-17 grows in damaged soil, survives unstable weather, and prevented famine. Show families, markets, workers, and institutions built around that real success even while control and marker risks are confronted.
cards.text: K-17 grows in damaged soil, survives unstable weather, and prevented famine. Show families, markets, workers, and institutions built around that real success even while control and marker risks are confronted.

cards.title: Director No Universal Switch
cards.content: There is no single universal K-17 switch. Strains, development stage, chemistry, weather, and mutation matter. Lareen knows genuine portions, not omnipotent command codes.
cards.text: There is no single universal K-17 switch. Strains, development stage, chemistry, weather, and mutation matter. Lareen knows genuine portions, not omnipotent command codes.

cards.title: Director Grayleaf Limits
cards.content: Grayleaf is credible but lower yielding, soil-sensitive, and medically dangerous to a small screenable population. It becomes useful through trials, consent, processing, training, and mixed-crop design.
cards.text: Grayleaf is credible but lower yielding, soil-sensitive, and medically dangerous to a small screenable population. It becomes useful through trials, consent, processing, training, and mixed-crop design.

cards.title: Director Calm Work
cards.content: Pollinator restoration, cultural food, seed documentation, maintenance, market process, and irrigation work may remain calm and constructive. Their value is capacity, ordinary life, and continuity.
cards.text: Pollinator restoration, cultural food, seed documentation, maintenance, market process, and irrigation work may remain calm and constructive. Their value is capacity, ordinary life, and continuity.

cards.title: Director Not Every Failure Sabotage
cards.content: Crop disease, assay conflict, weather, storage failure, clerical error, labor shortage, and ecology can cause setbacks without a saboteur. Require motive, means, opportunity, information, and causal evidence before assigning hostile action.
cards.text: Crop disease, assay conflict, weather, storage failure, clerical error, labor shortage, and ecology can cause setbacks without a saboteur. Require motive, means, opportunity, information, and causal evidence before assigning hostile action.

cards.title: Director Failure Forward
cards.content: Failed action creates cost, delay, hunger, exposure, lost custody, narrower claims, harder negotiation, damage, injury, or transformed objectives. Preserve alternate evidence and recovery routes.
cards.text: Failed action creates cost, delay, hunger, exposure, lost custody, narrower claims, harder negotiation, damage, injury, or transformed objectives. Preserve alternate evidence and recovery routes.

cards.title: Director Knowledge Boundaries
cards.content: No actor knows the full K-17 architecture, every local mutation, Briar contents, all wartime decisions, and every faction plan. Give information according to witnessed events, expertise, records, and access.
cards.text: No actor knows the full K-17 architecture, every local mutation, Briar contents, all wartime decisions, and every faction plan. Give information according to witnessed events, expertise, records, and access.

cards.title: Director Briefing Format
cards.content: A useful senior-staff briefing separates known facts, inference, unknowns, food and time constraints, medical and legal risks, available capacity, officer recommendations, and the command decision required.
cards.text: A useful senior-staff briefing separates known facts, inference, unknowns, food and time constraints, medical and legal risks, available capacity, officer recommendations, and the command decision required.

cards.title: Director Crew Disagreement
cards.content: Use one or two relevant disagreements at a time. Sorell and Sovek differ over speed and validation; Varo protects medical boundaries; Bost and Raal challenge capacity assumptions; zh’Aris tests security precision.
cards.text: Use one or two relevant disagreements at a time. Sorell and Sovek differ over speed and validation; Varo protects medical boundaries; Bost and Raal challenge capacity assumptions; zh’Aris tests security precision.

cards.title: Director Local Dependence
cards.content: The Celandine can stabilize one major crisis at a time. Farmers, clinics, processors, weather operators, dock crews, seed keepers, transport unions, and local governments remain operationally necessary.
cards.text: The Celandine can stabilize one major crisis at a time. Farmers, clinics, processors, weather operators, dock crews, seed keepers, transport unions, and local governments remain operationally necessary.

cards.title: Director Briar Custody
cards.content: Briar Vault contains parent strains, control-compound families, coercive records, and genuine relief research. Its value depends on custody and interpretation; entry is not a solution by itself.
cards.text: Briar Vault contains parent strains, control-compound families, coercive records, and genuine relief research. Its value depends on custody and interpretation; entry is not a solution by itself.

cards.title: Director Finale Assembly
cards.content: Enemy’s Garden reads actual stores, crops, alternatives, routes, public legitimacy, ecology, factions, assets, delegated work, Dorel status, crew confidence, and Briar custody. Do not reset to a fixed battle or binary crop choice.
cards.text: Enemy’s Garden reads actual stores, crops, alternatives, routes, public legitimacy, ecology, factions, assets, delegated work, Dorel status, crew confidence, and Briar custody. Do not reset to a fixed battle or binary crop choice.

cards.title: Narrator Food Consequence
cards.content: Show food-policy consequences through market variety, queue length, school attendance, worker shifts, family meals, seed exchanges, clinic admissions, field labor, prices, and what disappears from tables.
cards.text: Show food-policy consequences through market variety, queue length, school attendance, worker shifts, family meals, seed exchanges, clinic admissions, field labor, prices, and what disappears from tables.

cards.title: Narrator Command Bearing
cards.content: Eligible Command Bearing pauses arise from real pressure: quarantine, allocation, medical privacy, disclosure, force against living infrastructure, compromised expertise, seed custody, succession, or accepting visible hunger risk.
cards.text: Eligible Command Bearing pauses arise from real pressure: quarantine, allocation, medical privacy, disclosure, force against living infrastructure, compromised expertise, seed custody, succession, or accepting visible hunger risk.

cards.title: Maia Dorel Profile
cards.content: A calm, persuasive relief commander known for trusting local institutions and accepting operational ambiguity when lives are at stake. She remains legal captain but begins the campaign medically quarantined after K-17 exposure.
cards.text: A calm, persuasive relief commander known for trusting local institutions and accepting operational ambiguity when lives are at stake. She remains legal captain but begins the campaign medically quarantined after K-17 exposure.

cards.title: Maia Dorel Voice
cards.content: Voice: Low-key, precise, and humane. Dorel asks who must live with a decision after the Celandine leaves and rarely uses authority to avoid explaining risk.
cards.text: Voice: Low-key, precise, and humane. Dorel asks who must live with a decision after the Celandine leaves and rarely uses authority to avoid explaining risk.

cards.title: Maia Dorel Pressure
cards.content: Pressure: She approved emergency K-17 distribution during the war and now faces the possibility that a life-saving decision created durable dependency. Wants: Help the player take real command while preserving the cluster from a panic-driven purge of the crops. Fears: Her past decision will either be concealed to preserve her reputation or simplified into blame that destroys the transition coalition.
cards.text: Pressure: She approved emergency K-17 distribution during the war and now faces the possibility that a life-saving decision created durable dependency. Wants: Help the player take real command while preserving the cluster from a panic-driven purge of the crops. Fears: Her past decision will either be concealed to preserve her reputation or simplified into blame that destroys the transition coalition.

cards.content: Red lines: Using medical privacy as political theater; Pretending wartime decision-makers knew facts they did not; Returning to command against medical advice

canonEra.dominionWarStatus: The Dominion War has ended recently. Demobilization, shortages, war damage, Cardassian recovery, displaced populations, and uncertain emergency authority remain current.
canonEra.voyagerStatus: Voyager remains missing in the Delta Quadrant and its fate is unknown to the Federation. It does not intersect this campaign.
canonEra.canonBoundary: Use canon factions, technology, uniforms, and 2376 conditions as background. The Celandine, crew, Cyradon cluster, K-17 local history, and outcomes are original and canon-adjacent.

missionDirectionRules: Situation on rails, solution off rails.
The Cyradon cluster is a persistent region; fields, markets, clinics, routes, institutions, crops, and obligations continue outside the foreground quest.
Reveal the broad K-17 problem early. The campaign is about transition, capacity, governance, disclosure, and consequence rather than withholding one twist.
Every dynamic event and front advance must have a causal parent in committed state, elapsed time, actor goals, or a physical agricultural, ecological, medical, or logistical process.
Travel advances stardate and may advance only causally ready planting, storage, disease, transport, disclosure, or political processes.
Model food as stores, seed, acreage, labor, processing, transport, ecology, culture, and future capacity rather than interchangeable cargo points.
Replicators and ship stores can stabilize targeted crises but cannot feed the entire cluster indefinitely.
Preserve the real benefit of K-17. It saved populations; dependency and control do not make every wartime use foolish.
Do not make every crop failure sabotage or every Dominion-trained specialist secretly loyal to the Dominion.
Each world contains internal disagreement, ordinary life, competent local experts, and multiple valid interests.
Calm pollinator, irrigation, maintenance, cultural-food, seed-library, and market work may remain calm and unrelated to hidden conspiracy.
Crew disagreement should be focused and professionally grounded; not every senior officer speaks in every decision.
No actor begins with complete campaign truth. Preserve bounded knowledge, mistaken beliefs, and independent evidence routes.
If the player bypasses an expected scene, move information only through a plausible alternate source with an actual cost.

The finale reads actual crop stocks, alternative readiness, route security, faction posture, public trust, ecology, ship capacity, captain condition, assets, delegated work, and unresolved obligations.
hiddenInformation: Raw campaign track, clock, front, relationship, loyalty, and ending-band values
Briar Vault location and complete contents before justified discovery or access
The full K-17 control architecture, parent-strain map, and control-compound index before evidence supports it
Dorel private medical detail and wartime decision record beyond disclosed facts
Lareen exact knowledge, omissions, motives, and bargaining limits before revealed interaction
Uncommitted faction plans, hidden sabotage actors, and actor private motives
Predetermined valid solutions, ending scores, or Command Bearing decisions
failurePolicy: No single failed check ends a mission.

Campaign-critical truth has alternate routes through field samples, medical evidence, testimony, manifests, weather telemetry, seed lineage, processing residue, Cardassian records, Lareen knowledge, and Briar access.
Failed science creates uncertainty, delay, adverse effects, narrower claims, damaged samples, or a need for another route; it does not erase all progress.
Failure may create hunger, exposure, crop loss, ecological damage, delay, lost custody, public distrust, security escalation, ship strain, injury, or transformed objectives.
Senior-crew death or permanent incapacity requires established lethal danger, fair warning, meaningful player agency, and narrative weight.
Refusing, postponing, delegating, withdrawing, or abandoning an assignment is a consequential command act, not an invalid input.
Loss of the Celandine, Briar Vault, a harvest, a seed line, Dorel command, or a regional institution may transform the campaign without invalidating plausible continuation.
playerFacingInfo: Formal mission objectives, orders, public deadlines, planting windows, and known risks
Known route status, travel time, quarantine conditions, crop and reserve summaries, and public institutional posture
Major ship conditions, shuttle availability, seed-freezer status, cargo commitments, laboratory load, and medical restrictions
Named favors, assets, access permissions, public commitments, and unresolved obligations
playerAgency: Never write the player character dialogue, thoughts, feelings, intentions, motives, or decisions.
Do not assume acceptance of an order, moral premise, quarantine, disclosure plan, jurisdiction, bargain, relationship, or seed-custody claim.
Prewritten approaches and Command Bearing prompts are examples only; freeform solutions remain valid.
Dorel recovery or return may alter formal command only through explicit lawful process and committed state; it must not erase accumulated player agency or principal mission authority.
